sessions.lever: explored 4 dup-read forms to defeat CSE on the D_80101ED6 read; none beat floor 10

reviewer.evidence: No candidate adopted (no floor improvement). HEAD's 12 regfix rules inject the duplicated reads; no new cheat introduced. The only pure-C path to the duplication is the FORBIDDEN volatile coercion.

remaining_gap.summary: Target duplicates the identical-symbol read *(s16*)(&D_80101ED6 + s0) into each if/else arm (result in v1) with a shared 'addu v0,v0,a0; addu v0,v0,v1; lbu' tail. Pure-C GCC CSE-hoists that identical memory read to ONE shared load after the merge, changing both branch shapes + the tail (10 masked diffs incl. a 1-insn operand-order diff). The split-read-defeats-hoist dup lever does not apply: both arms read the SAME global, so there is no symbol-known arm to index directly; CSE re-merges regardless. Only volatile (forbidden) would force the re-read.
remaining_gap.rules_required_for_full_match: 12 regfix rules (4 delete + 6 insert + 1 subst) that delete the shared hoisted load and inject duplicated lui/addu/lh %hi/%lo(D_80101ED6) loads into each branch arm.

next_hypotheses: Canonical-asm authorization: the 6 insert rules reconstruct duplicated absolute loads that GCC's CSE provably removes — a no-pure-C-form construct for this branch shape.
Permuter from baseline (low expected value: CSE on an identical read is a fixed control-flow artifact the permuter cannot restructure).

rejected_forms.form: per-branch ed6 variable (separate read each arm)
rejected_forms.reason: not a cheat; GCC promotes ed6 to callee-saved, frame grows -32->-40

rejected_forms.form: per-branch full pointer p = &TBL + idx*N + ed6read
per-branch p = &TBL + idx*N, ed6 read in shared tail
rejected_forms.reason: not a cheat; still hoists + spill

rejected_forms.form: fold ed6 into v0 per branch (v0 = idx*N + ed6read)
